slides.title: Why V4 Needs Presentation Packaging
slides.headline: Readable docs solved traceability, but leadership alignment still needs a governed narrative and rendering layer.
slides.core_message: Readable docs solved traceability, but leadership alignment still needs a governed narrative and rendering layer.
supporting_points: The workspace now has a readable discovery, strategy, prototype, roadmap, and PRD document bundle.
The doc bundle is usable but still carries targeted review and publishing-metadata follow-up work.
PMs still need a single repo-native way to declare the mission, capture the first few high-signal questions, and steer ProductOS through the full PM loop.
PMs still need a single repo-native way to declare the mission, capture the first few high-signal questions, and steer ProductOS through the full PM loop.
slides.speaker_notes: Open with the decision and make clear that this is a communication-system upgrade, not a styling request. Claim posture: observed facts support the current narrative. Validation next: Taken directly from the canonical mission brief. Taken directly from the canonical mission brief. Keep these proof gaps visible: Keep the mission 'PM superpower recovery mission' visible across docs and deck so PM intent does not get flattened into generic release narration..
slides.audience_question_answered: What decision should leadership make now that the readable-doc layer exists?
slides.claim: ProductOS should approve a governed presentation layer so readable docs can become leadership-ready communication without manual PM rewriting.
slides.why_now: Leadership should approve the next bounded communication slice and understand why it is a governance upgrade rather than cosmetic polish. The audience should also understand how this package advances the mission 'PM superpower recovery mission'. The audience should also understand how this package advances the mission 'PM superpower recovery mission'.
slides.proof_strategy: State the decision clearly; Show that the readable-doc system is real but not yet enough for executive alignment; Claim posture: Claim posture: observed facts support the current narrative.

slides.title: What Keeps This Safe
slides.headline: Narrative power must stay subordinate to evidence, validation, and manual approval.
slides.core_message: Narrative power must stay subordinate to evidence, validation, and manual approval.
supporting_points: Internal adoption was allowed only because the unresolved publishing and pattern-governance questions stayed explicit.
The doc bundle is usable but still carries targeted review and publishing-metadata follow-up work.
The workspace now has a readable discovery, strategy, prototype, roadmap, and PRD document bundle.
Outcome review says the readable-doc bundle improved internal reviewability but still needs another iteration for external-ready communication.
Keep the mission 'PM superpower recovery mission' visible across docs and deck so PM intent does not get flattened into generic release narration.
slides.speaker_notes: Close by showing that the proposal is disciplined and supervised. Claim posture: observed facts support the current narrative. slides.audience_question_answered: How do we prevent a stronger presentation system from turning into ungoverned persuasion?
slides.claim: Presentation packaging is acceptable only if audience policy, pattern review, publish checks, and manual validation stay explicit.
slides.why_now: Leadership should approve the next bounded communication slice and understand why it is a governance upgrade rather than cosmetic polish. The audience should also understand how this package advances the mission 'PM superpower recovery mission'. slides.proof_strategy: Show the required control layers; Show which gaps remain blocked until pattern governance is in place; Claim posture: Claim posture: observed facts support the current narrative.

slides.cta: Treat pattern review and publish checks as part of the implementation, not post-work.
